title: High-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product having a reduced viscosity, method of producing it, and related ingredients
abstract: The present invention relates to a new method of producing a high-protein, acidified, liquid, dairy product. The invention furthermore relates to new high-protein acidified, liquid dairy product, to protein/mineral compositions and uses of these for producing liquid dairy products having a high-protein content and a low viscosity.
claims: Claims 1. A method of preparing a high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product, comprising the steps of a) providing a liquid composition - comprising a total amount of whey protein of at least 4% (w/w) of which at least 30%(w/w) is in the form of insoluble whey protein particles, - having volume weighted mean particle diameter, D[4,3], of at most 50 micron, - wherein at least 90% (w/w) of the total protein is whey protein, b) subjecting the liquid composition of step a) to at least one acidification step using an acidifying agent and to at least one heat-treatment step in any order, thereby obtaining an acidified heat-treated liquid composition, c) optionally, homogenizing the acidified heat-treated liquid composition, and d) optionally, packaging an acidified dairy product derived from the acidified, heat-treated liquid composition of step b) or step c), wherein Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations are present prior to step b and/or added during or after step b) in an amount sufficient to obtain a weight ratio between the total amount of protein and the total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations of at most 100 in the acidified, heat-treated, liquid composition. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the total amount of whey protein is at least 6% (w/w). 3. The method according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the total amount of whey protein is in the range of 4-25% (w/w). 4. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the whey protein comprises at least 40%(w/w) insoluble whey protein particles, preferably at least 50% (w/w), even more preferably at least 60% (w/w). 5. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the total amount of protein is at least 4% (w/w). 6. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the total amount of protein is in the range of 4-25% (w/w). 7. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the total amount of protein comprises at least 85% (w/w) whey protein. 8. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations is at least 100 mg/100 ml. 9. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the weight ratio between total protein and total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations is at most 90, preferably at most 50. 10. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid composition of step a) furthermore comprises lipid. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein the lipid comprises milk lipid and/or vegetable lipid. 12. The method according to claims 10 or 11, wherein the liquid composition comprises one or more milk lipid sources, e.g. selected from the group consisting of cream, butter, butter fat, anhydrous milk fat, whey fat, and combinations thereof. 13. The method according to any of the claims 10-12, wherein the liquid composition of step a) comprises lipid in an amount of at most 3% (w/w). 14. The method according to any of the claims 10-12, wherein the liquid composition of step a) comprises lipid in an amount of at least 4% (w/w). 15. The method according to any of the claims 10-12, wherein the liquid composition of step a) comprises lipid in an amount of in the range of 4-20% (w/w). 16. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid composition of step a) furthermore comprises carbohydrate. 17. The method according to claim 16, wherein the carbohydrate comprises di- and/or monosaccharide. 18. The method according to any of claims 16 or 17, wherein the total amount of carbohydrates is at least 5%(w/w). 19. The method according to any of claims 16 or 17, wherein the total amount of carbohydrates is at most 4%(w/w) . 20. The method according to any of any of claims 16-19, wherein the carbohydrate comprises lactose. 21. The method according to claim 20, wherein the total amount of lactose is at least 5%(w/w). 22. The method according to claim 20, wherein the total amount of lactose is at most 4%. 23. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid composition of step a) furthermore comprises one or more vitamin(s) and similar other ingredients, such as vitamin A, vitamin D, vitamin E, vitamin K, thiamine, riboflavin, pyridoxine, vitamin B12, niacin, folic acid, pantothenic acid, biotin, vitamin C, choline, vitamin B8, their salts, their derivatives and combinations thereof. 24. The method according to claim 23, wherein the vitamin comprises or even consists essentially of vitamin D. 25. The method according to any of the preceding claim, wherein the liquid composition of step a) comprises an amount of total solids in the range of 4-50% (w/wj. 26. The method according to claim 25, wherein the liquid composition of step a) comprises an amount of total solids in the range of 28-36% (w/w). 27. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid composition of step a) furthermore comprises one or more non-carbohydrate natural or artificial sweetener. 28. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the liquid composition of step a) furthermore comprises carbohydrate-based stabilisers. 29. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one heat- treatment step of step b) comprises heating to a temperature of at least 60 degrees C for a duration sufficient to obtain at least a 5-logio reduction of viable Escherichia coli. 30. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one heat treatment step comprises pre-acidification heat treatment and/or post acidification heat treatment. 31. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein: - step b) comprises subjecting the liquid composition to pre-acidification heat treatment followed by an acidification step, or - step b) comprises subjecting the liquid composition to pre-acidification heat treatment followed by an acidification step followed by post acidification heat treatment, or - step b) comprises subjecting the liquid composition to an acidification step followed by post acidification heat treatment. 32. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the heat treatment(s) of step b) is followed by cooling the liquid composition to a temperature at most 50 degrees C. 33. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the acidifying agent comprises a bacterial culture. 34. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the acidifying agent comprises a chemical acidifying agent. 35. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the acidifying agent reduces pH of the liquid composition of step b) to a pH of at most 5.5. 36. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the homogenization step d) involves a pressure drop of at least 20 bar. 37. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the packaging step e) comprises any suitable packaging technique, such as e.g. aseptic conditions and use of any suitable container. 38. A high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product obtainable by the method according to any of claims 1-37. 39. A high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product: - comprising a total amount of whey protein of at least 4% (w/w) of which at least 30% is in the form of insoluble whey protein particles, - having volume weighted mean particle diameter, D[4,3], of at most 50 micron, - wherein at least 90% (w/w) of the total protein is whey protein, and - having a weight ratio between the total amount protein and the total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations of at most 100. 40. A high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product according to claim 38 or 39, wherein the total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations is at least 100 mg/100 ml. 41. The high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product according to any of the claims 38-40 having a viscosity of at most 2500cP at 5 degrees C at a shear rate of 300/s. 42. The high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product according to any of claims 38-41, wherein the high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product is yoghurt, such as e.g. a stirred yoghurt or a drinking yoghurt. 43. A protein/mineral powder - comprising a total amount of at least 20% (w/w) whey protein of which at least 30% (w/w) is in the form of insoluble whey protein particles, - having volume weighted mean particle diameter, D[4,3], of at most 50 micron when suspended in water, - wherein at least 90% (w/w) of the total protein is whey protein, and - having a weight ratio between the total amount of protein and the total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations of at most 100. 44. Use of the protein/mineral powder according to any of claim 43 for producing a high- protein, acidified, liquid dairy product having a viscosity of at most 2500cP at 5 degrees C and at a shear rate of 300/s. 45. The use according to claim 44, wherein the high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product: - comprises a total amount of at least 4% (w/w) whey protein of which at least 30% is in the form of insoluble whey protein particles - has a volume weighted mean particle diameter, D[4,3], of at most 50 micron, and - has a weight ratio between the total amount of protein and the total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations of at most 100, 46. The use according to any of claims 44 or 45 wherein the high-protein, acidified, liquid dairy product comprises a total amount of Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ cations of at least 100 mg/100 ml.
